biomarkers.source_anchor: ...mortality in these cases was the lowest of all the different causes of sepsis (13.6%). This finding is consistent with results reported from a population-based study conducted in the USA in which urinary tract infection was considered a protective factor against death (adjusted OR = 0.3).
biomarkers.auditor_reasoning: The quote states that urinary tract infection has an adjusted Odds Ratio of 0.3, which directly supports the extracted fact.

biomarkers.source_anchor: Mortality is significantly high (57.3%) for patients with ≥3 organ dysfunctions.

biomarkers.source_anchor: Vital signs such as higher heart rate (118 vs 106, _P_ <0.001) and respiratory rate (27 vs 24), _P_ <0.001) and lower systolic blood pressure (112 vs 106, _P_ <0.001) and oxygen saturation (92 vs 96), _P_ <0.014) were significant in mortality.
biomarkers.auditor_reasoning: The quote explicitly states that the higher heart rate has a p-value of <0.001, directly supporting the extracted fact.
